method_atom: A large-deviation and extreme-value analysis of the maximum and minimum of a large set of strongly correlated random variables whose joint density carries a pairwise logarithmic (Coulomb-gas) repulsion, using a saddle-point/variational treatment of the equilibrium density to obtain the asymptotic probability of atypically large fluctuations of the extremal variable. The same variational machinery yields the equilibrium density under an interval constraint that confines all variables to a bounded window, generalizing the unconstrained semicircular equilibrium law and producing a generic inverse-square-root edge singularity at the imposed barriers, with the leading large-system probability decaying as the exponential of a universal constant times the square of the system size.
object_atom: The eigenvalues of large N-by-N Gaussian random matrices in the orthogonal, unitary, and symplectic ensembles, indexed by the Dyson symmetry parameter.
brief_reason: The Coulomb-gas large-deviation and constrained-equilibrium-density machinery for logarithmically-repelling correlated variables transfers across fields, while the Gaussian random-matrix eigenvalue spectrum is the domain-specific object.
